title: jest-axe — Custom Jest Matcher for axe-core
insights: toHaveNoViolations Jest custom matcher. Wraps axe-core for unit tests. Color contrast checks disabled in JSDOM (known limitation). Add toHaveNoViolations to every component test for regression prevention. Color contrast must be tested separately in browser.

title: Automated Accessibility Testing with axe-core, Playwright & GitHub Actions
insights: Complete CI pipeline: install Playwright, run axe via @axe-core/playwright, generate JSON reports, create GitHub Issue on violations. Targets WCAG 2.1 AA by default. Practical GitHub Actions YAML included.

title: Accessibility Testing — Playwright Docs
insights: Official Playwright + axe-core integration. @axe-core/playwright package. AxeBuilder API. checkA11y() in tests. Snapshots for violation tracking. Integration into CI/CD straightforward.

title: eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y — GitHub
insights: Static AST checker for JSX a11y rules. plugin:jsx-a11y/recommended for standard rules, /strict for stricter subset. Catches missing alt text, invalid ARIA roles, missing form labels at lint time. Use alongside axe-core — static + dynamic coverage.

title: Accessibility audit with react-axe and eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y — web.dev
insights: Google's guide on layered a11y tooling: eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y (static) + @axe-core/react (runtime in browser) + jest-axe (test time). Three layers catch different issue types. Essential shift-left strategy.

title: React Aria — Adobe
insights: 50+ hooks implementing WAI-ARIA patterns. Normalizes browser/AT differences. Full keyboard and screen reader support. Style-agnostic — works with Tailwind, CSS modules, etc. usePress, useFocusRing, useInteractOutside, useFocusWithin hooks highly reusable.

title: adobe/react-spectrum — GitHub
insights: Full monorepo including React Aria, React Spectrum, React Stately. Adobe's production accessible component system. Built to WCAG + WAI-ARIA APG patterns. Cross-browser AT normalization built in.

title: Accessibility — Radix Primitives
insights: Radix follows WAI-ARIA APG patterns. All primitives include aria attributes, keyboard navigation, focus management. Tested across modern browsers and assistive technologies. Used as base for shadcn/ui.

title: Headless UI alternatives: Radix vs React Aria vs Ark UI — LogRocket
insights: Comparison matrix. Radix: mature, ready-to-use. React Aria: maximum control, hook-based. Ark UI: newer, Zag.js state machines. All implement WAI-ARIA. Choose React Aria if a11y is primary concern.

title: Assistiv Labs — Cloud Screen Reader Testing
insights: Real screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, Narrator, ZoomText) in cloud. Run on Mac/PC/Linux. Can automate screen reader interactions. Finds 75% of bugs that automated checkers miss. Used by Asana for E2E a11y testing.

title: Which Screen Readers and Browsers are Best for Accessibility Testing? — Assistiv Labs
insights: JAWS+Chrome, NVDA+Chrome, JAWS+Edge, NVDA+Firefox are the dominant pairings. 90% of usage split across NVDA+JAWS+VoiceOver. Recommends testing NVDA+Chrome + VoiceOver+Safari minimum.

title: Screen Reader Testing Guide: NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver — TestParty
insights: Testing matrix: NVDA (Windows, free, code issues), JAWS (Windows, enterprise, user experience), VoiceOver (macOS/iOS). Browse/Virtual mode vs Focus/Forms mode distinction critical. Test at least 2 screen readers.

title: Foundations: Form Validation and Error Messages — TetraLogical (Oct 2024)
insights: Accessible errors: aria-invalid='true' on field, aria-describedby linking to error message. Error summary at top with links for WCAG 2.2. In-line errors appear after focus leaves field. Text-based, not color-only.

title: Building a Culture of Accessibility — TetraLogical (Apr 2024)
insights: Organizational approach: accessible onboarding, training for managers, inclusive policies. A11y as continuous culture not one-off audit. Practical org-level recommendations.

title: Design Patterns and WCAG — TetraLogical (Aug 2024)
insights: Clarifies that APG patterns are best-practice guidance, NOT WCAG conformance requirements. WCAG is technology-neutral — the how (ARIA vs native HTML) is flexible as long as the outcome is accessible.

title: Making Content Usable for People with Cognitive and Learning Disabilities — W3C
insights: COGA supplemental guidance. 8 objectives beyond WCAG AA. Covers cognitive load, consistent navigation, help for errors, no unusual words. WCAG 2.2 AA weak for cognitive disabilities — COGA fills the gap. Used in WCAG 3.0 development.

title: Cognitive Accessibility at W3C — WAI
insights: Hub page for COGA task force resources, WCAG 2.2 cognitive criteria, and planned WCAG 3.0 cognitive outcomes. Links to 'Making Content Usable' and pattern library.

title: An Alt Decision Tree — WAI/W3C
insights: Decision tree for choosing correct alt text: decorative (alt=''), functional (describe purpose), informative (describe content), complex (long description needed), text in images (transcribe). Essential for content authors and developers.

title: Tables Tutorial — WAI/W3C
insights: Progressive complexity: simple (scope), multi-level (headers attribute), irregular (id+headers). caption for table title. scope='col'/'row'/'colgroup'/'rowgroup'. All th should have scope.

title: User Notification (Forms) — WAI/W3C Tutorial
insights: In-page notification for form errors. On-submit: focus error summary, list links to each invalid field. aria-invalid marks fields. role=alert for immediate errors. Official W3C tutorial.

title: A Step-By-Step Guide to Building Accessible Carousels — Smashing Magazine
insights: Comprehensive implementation guide. aria-roledescription, pause controls mandatory for auto-advance, aria-hidden on inactive slides. Semantic HTML + CSS scroll for performance. Covers WCAG 2.5.2 (no activation on pointer release) considerations.

title: VPAT — Information Technology Industry Council (ITI)
insights: VPAT 2.5 (2023) aligned to WCAG 2.2. 4 editions: 508, EU, WCAG, INT. Completed VPAT = Accessibility Conformance Report (ACR). Required for US federal procurement. Enterprise accessibility due diligence.

title: ACR/VPAT FAQ — Section508.gov
insights: US government procurement guidance. Federal agencies must buy accessible ICT per Section 508. ACR/VPAT is the evaluation mechanism. Key for SaaS products targeting government customers.

title: WCAG 3.0 Explained: What the Future Means for You — AudioEye
insights: WCAG 3.0 Working Draft Sept 2025. Replaces A/AA/AAA with Bronze/Silver/Gold. Shifts from binary pass/fail to scored outcomes. Still years from official recommendation. WCAG 2.2 remains the legal standard.

title: WCAG 3.0 Proposed Scoring Model: A Shift in Accessibility Evaluation — Smashing Magazine
insights: Deep analysis of WCAG 3.0 scoring vs WCAG 2.x binary model. WCAG 3.0 prioritizes usability over compliance. Shift from 'presence of features' to 'quality of access'. Not backward compatible. Important for strategic planning.

title: WCAG 2.2 Checklist: Complete 2026 Compliance Guide — Level Access
insights: All 87 WCAG 2.2 success criteria organized by level. 32 Level A + 24 Level AA + 31 Level AAA. Practical implementation checklist format. Good QA gate reference.

title: What Are All 87 WCAG 2.2 Success Criteria? — TestParty
insights: Complete flat list of all 87 criteria with level. Useful for building automated test matrices and mapping tools to criteria. Quick reference for team onboarding.

title: New Success Criteria in WCAG 2.2 — TPGi (Vispero)
insights: Deep technical analysis of all 9 new SC. Steve Faulkner contributor analysis. Includes implementation notes and edge cases for each criterion.

title: RGAA Compliance: Complete Guide to French Digital Accessibility 2025 — DigitalA11Y
insights: RGAA 4.1.2 = 106 criteria based on WCAG 2.1. Less than 10% of French sites fully compliant in 2024. Penalties up to €50k since Jan 2024. Accessibility declaration mandatory. Private sector obligations extended from June 2025 (EAA transposition).

title: EU Accessibility Act in France: Law 2005-102 & RGAA — Quertum
insights: French Law 2005-102 as legal basis for RGAA. How EAA transposition extends obligations to private sector. French-specific enforcement context. CNIL role in data aspects.

title: New Class Action Lawsuit against AccessiBe — Law Office of Lainey Feingold (Jul 2024)
insights: July 2024 class action. AccessiBe overlay failed to provide compliance despite claims. FTC fined AccessiBe $1M Jan 2025 for false advertising. 25% of all ADA lawsuits in 2024 cited overlays as barriers. Critical warning against overlay-as-compliance.

title: Accessibility Overlay Widgets Attract Lawsuits — Accessibility.Works
insights: Technical analysis of why overlays fail. Even the best automated software catches only 30% of WCAG guidelines. Overlays often introduce NEW barriers. Real remediation requires manual code fixes.

title: The Anatomy of Accessible Forms: Error Messages — Deque
insights: aria-invalid='true', aria-describedby pointing to error. Error text must be next to or linked to the field. Do not rely on color alone. Error summary at top with anchor links to fields. Deque's canonical guide.

title: Breadcrumb Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: nav element with aria-label='Breadcrumb'. Ordered list structure. Current page link has aria-current='page'. Visual separators via CSS only (not in DOM) to avoid screen reader verbosity.

title: ARIA: aria-current attribute — MDN Web Docs
insights: Values: page, step, location, date, time, true. aria-current='page' for breadcrumbs and nav items. Announced by screen readers as 'current'. Distinct from aria-selected (used in list/tree contexts).

title: ARIA: tablist role — MDN Web Docs
insights: role=tablist container, role=tab for each tab, role=tabpanel for content. Active tab tabindex=0, inactive tabindex=-1. Arrow keys between tabs. Tab key enters panel. Home/End to first/last.

title: ARIA: aria-busy attribute — MDN Web Docs
insights: aria-busy=true signals content loading. Do NOT use on buttons (inconsistent screen reader support). Use with aria-live region for spinner state. Set to false when content loads. Pair with role=status for polite announcement.

title: ARIA: tooltip role — MDN Web Docs
insights: role=tooltip + aria-describedby on trigger. Visible on hover and keyboard focus. Escape dismisses. Do not use HTML title attribute (inconsistently supported). WCAG 1.4.13: content visible on hover must be persistent and hoverable.

title: Tooltip Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Official pattern. Tooltip appears on focus AND hover. Tooltip itself is NOT focusable. aria-describedby links trigger to tooltip. Dismiss with Escape. Persistent on hover (WCAG 1.4.13). Popover for interactive content.

title: Understanding SC 2.4.7: Focus Visible — WAI/W3C
insights: AA level (WCAG 2.1). Focus indicator must be visible — browser default is acceptable but often insufficient. 2.4.13 (AAA) adds specifics: 2px thick, 3:1 contrast. Never remove outline without replacement.

title: Pope Tech — Enterprise Accessibility Platform (WAVE-powered)
insights: Enterprise-scale WAVE scanning. Automated Accessibility Score vs WebAIM Million baseline. Scheduled scans, dashboards, role-based reports. Used by universities, governments, large organizations. Updated WAVE engine in 2024.

title: WAVE Web Accessibility Evaluation Tools — WebAIM
insights: Free browser extension + API for accessibility evaluation. Visual overlay showing errors, alerts, structural elements, ARIA. 1M+ pages scanned monthly via Pope Tech. Complementary to axe-core — different rule emphasis.

title: Lighthouse Accessibility Score — Chrome for Developers
insights: Weighted average of all axe-based audits. Pass/fail only — no partial credit. Set baseline >90 in LHCI config. Lighthouse 12 (May 2024) removed PWA category. LHCI 0.15.x uses Lighthouse 12.6.1.

title: Make Accessible Carousels — Chrome for Developers
insights: Modern approach using CSS scroll-state and scroll-marker-group. Performance-first carousels that are accessible at first paint. Combining semantic HTML, CSS interactivity, and ARIA for progressively enhanced carousels.

title: WCAG 2.2 Complete Compliance Guide 2025 — AllAccessible
insights: 2025 implementation guidance for all 9 new WCAG 2.2 criteria with code examples. Practical focus on what changed from 2.1 to 2.2 for development teams.

title: PDF/UA Standard Explained — PDF Tools
insights: PDF/UA (ISO 14289) structure and tagging requirements. PDF/UA-2 published 2024 based on PDF 2.0. All meaningful content in single structure tree. PAC 2024 checker from PDF/UA Foundation. Distinct from but complementary to WCAG.

title: WCAG and PDF/UA in Focus for PDF Accessibility — axes4 (2024)
insights: 2024 analysis of how WCAG and PDF/UA overlap and differ. PDF/UA-2 (2024) as complete rewrite based on PDF 2.0. PAC 2024 checks WCAG 2.1 PDF criteria. Essential for enterprise document accessibility.
